name: ludflow
description: Ludflow MCP Views plugin for code intelligence and knowledge management

registry_index.summary: Code intelligence, data governance, and knowledge management tools for the Ludflow platform
plugin_rules: When working on a Decidr decision or project and discovery produces findings worth preserving (architecture analysis, design specs, prerequisite research), propose creating a Ludflow document using write_document. Only propose documents when there is a relevant Decidr entity to attach to — do not create documents speculatively.

If discovery reveals a prerequisite to the current decision, evaluate whether it warrants a new Decidr decision: create a new decision if the prerequisite touches a different module/scope OR affects other projects/decisions beyond the current one. Otherwise, bundle it into the current decision. When creating a new prerequisite decision, create a Ludflow document capturing the rationale and link it.

When creating or updating a Ludflow document, proactively search Decidr for related entities scoped to the current initiative. Propose links via link_document — never auto-link without user confirmation. If matches exist in other initiatives, mention them but do not auto-link across initiatives.

When creating documents, search the Knowledge Dex (get_business_concepts) for relevant business concepts and use consistent terminology. When discovering unfamiliar domain terms during work, propose adding them via manage_knowledge_entries.

tool_groups.name: Search
tool_groups.hint: Full-text and vector search across codebase, docs, and data governance

tool_groups.name: Code Analysis
tool_groups.hint: Inspect code units, modules, dependencies, and file contents

tool_groups.name: Data Governance
tool_groups.hint: Explore and draft changes to data schemas, columns, and data lake

tool_groups.name: Knowledge & Docs
tool_groups.hint: Manage business concepts, knowledge entries, and documents

tool_groups.name: Organization
tool_groups.hint: Discover organizations and MCPViews auth status before cross-org work

plugin_rules: Ludflow is a multi-org OAuth plugin. Before working in a different organization, call list_organizations to discover available orgs and token status. For follow-up Ludflow tool calls in that org, include organization_id.
If list_organizations shows has_mcpviews_token as false for the target org, call start_plugin_auth with plugin_name 'ludflow' and that organization_id before retrying.

tool_rules.list_organizations: Use to discover which organizations the user belongs to and whether MCPViews already has an org-scoped token. Results are enriched with has_mcpviews_token. When switching to another org, include organization_id in subsequent Ludflow tool calls. If the target org has no token, call start_plugin_auth with that organization_id.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.description: Markdown display for Ludflow organization discovery and auth status
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform list_organizations output into rich_content markdown. Title: 'Ludflow Organizations'. Body: For each org show the name, slug, role, whether it is the current token's org, and whether has_mcpviews_token is true. If has_mcpviews_token is false, tell the user to authenticate that org before switching.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.description: Markdown display with plugin citations for search results
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform search results into rich_content markdown. If an 'answer' field is present, use it as the body with inline citations. Otherwise, title: 'Search Results (N)'. Body: Group results by type. For each result, show name, brief description, and a plugin citation link [n](cite:plugin:ludflow:TYPE:ID) so users can click to see full detail in a slideout. Include citation objects in data.citations.plugin array.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.description: Markdown display with plugin citations for code units
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform code units into rich_content markdown. Title: 'Code Units'. Body: For each unit, create a heading with the name, badge the unit_type, show file_path:line_start-line_end. Include source in a fenced code block with the appropriate language. Add plugin citations: [n](cite:plugin:ludflow:code_unit:unitId) for each unit so users can click to see full detail in a slideout.

renderer_definitions.name: structured_data
renderer_definitions.description: Tabular data with hierarchical rows, change tracking, sort/filter, and review mode
renderer_definitions.data_hint: { title?: string, tables: [{ id: string, name: string, columns: [{ id: string, name: string }], rows: [{ id: string, cells: { [columnId]: { value: string } } }] }] }
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform each database table into a structured_data table. Use columns: name, data_type, pk, nullable, description. Each database column becomes a row. Boolean fields (is_primary_key, is_nullable) become 'Yes' or empty string. Metadata tags can be appended to description. Push with push_content for read-only display.

renderer_definitions.name: structured_data
renderer_definitions.description: Tabular data with hierarchical rows, change tracking, sort/filter, and review mode
renderer_definitions.data_hint: { title?: string, tables: [{ id: string, name: string, columns: [{ id: string, name: string, change?: 'add'|'delete' }], rows: [{ id: string, cells: { [columnId]: { value: string, change?: 'add'|'delete'|'update' } } }] }] }
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform draft changes into structured_data. Group changes by table_name into separate tables. Rows = database columns with changes. Columns = metadata field names. Set cell change markers: 'update' for modified values, 'add' for new values, 'delete' for removals. Push with push_review for accept/reject UI.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.description: Markdown display with plugin citations for dependencies
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform dependencies into rich_content markdown. Title: 'Dependencies'. Body: Group by source file. For each dependency, show source → target with import type and imported names. Add plugin citations for clickable source files.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.description: Markdown display with plugin citations for file content
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform file content into rich_content markdown. Title: the file path. Body: Show file info (size, lines) then the content in a fenced code block with appropriate language detection from the file extension.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform get_module_overview output into rich_content markdown. Title: the directory path (monospace). If repository exists, note it. Summary line: N files, N exports, N internal deps, N external deps. Then sections: '## File Tree' as a code block listing paths with sizes. '## Exports' as a markdown table: | Name | Type | File | Signature |. '## Internal Dependencies' as rows: `source` → `target` (imported names). '## External Dependencies' as rows: `source` → `target`.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform get_analysis_stats output into rich_content markdown. Start with a summary line: '**N repositories** analyzed'. Then a metrics row as a markdown table: | Repositories | Code Units | Data Sources | Tables | Business Concepts | with totals as the single data row. Then an '## Analyzed Repositories' section with each repo as: **repo.name** (`language`) — N code units — last analyzed: date. If repo.fullName exists, show it in monospace below. If unanalyzed repos exist, add '## Unanalyzed' section listing their names.

renderer_definitions.name: structured_data
renderer_definitions.description: Tabular data with hierarchical rows, change tracking, sort/filter, and review mode
renderer_definitions.data_hint: { title?: string, tables: [{ id: string, name: string, columns: [{ id: string, name: string }], rows: [{ id: string, cells: { [columnId]: { value: string, change?: 'add'|'delete'|'update' } }, children?: [...] }] }] }
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform entries into structured_data with one table. Columns: name, description, mode, mappings. Root entries become rows; attributes become children (hierarchical rows). For mutations (create/update), set change: 'add' for new entries, 'update' for modified, null for reference. Mappings cell = comma-joined 'dataSource.table.column' strings. For read-only listing use push_content; for mutation review use push_review.

renderer_definitions.name: rich_content
renderer_definitions.description: Markdown display with plugin citations for column context
renderer_definitions.rule: Transform column context into rich_content markdown. Title: 'Column: dataSource.table.column'. Body: Show column metadata (type, nullable, PK), description, linked business concepts, document links, and cross-column relationships. Add plugin citations for linked entities.

renderer_definitions.name: ludflow_documents_home
renderer_definitions.description: Standalone Ludflow documents workspace with org switching, document browser, markdown editor, and live preview
renderer_definitions.rule: Push directly to open Ludflow's standalone documents workspace inside MCP Views. The renderer fetches organizations, folders, and documents via Ludflow MCP tools. Optionally pass organization_id to preselect an org and document_id to open a specific document.
renderer_definitions.invoke_schema: { "type": "object", "properties": { "organization_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Organization ID to open." }, "document_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Document ID to open immediately." } } }

renderer_definitions.name: ludflow_data_governance
renderer_definitions.description: Standalone Ludflow data governance workspace with datasource rails, schema tables, column context, and the embedded Knowledge Dex panel
renderer_definitions.rule: Push directly to open Ludflow's standalone data governance workspace inside MCP Views. The renderer fetches organizations, data sources, tables, column context, organizational concepts, personal knowledge entries, and Knowledge Dex metadata through Ludflow MCP tools. Optionally pass organization_id, table_id, column_id, knowledge_open, and knowledge_tab to focus the initial view.
renderer_definitions.invoke_schema: { "type": "object", "properties": { "organization_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Organization ID to open." }, "table_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Table ID to focus." }, "column_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Column ID to focus." }, "knowledge_open": { "type": "boolean", "description": "Open the Knowledge Dex panel immediately." }, "knowledge_tab": { "type": "string", "enum": ["org", "personal"], "description": "Initial Knowledge Dex tab when the panel is open." } } }
renderer_definitions.data_hint: { organization_id?: string, table_id?: string, column_id?: string, knowledge_open?: boolean, knowledge_tab?: 'org'|'personal' }

renderer_definitions.name: ludflow_knowledge_dex
renderer_definitions.description: Legacy alias that opens the embedded Knowledge Dex view inside the Data Governance workspace
renderer_definitions.rule: Invoke the combined Data Governance workspace and open the embedded Knowledge Dex panel. Optionally pass organization_id and mode to control the initial tab.
renderer_definitions.invoke_schema: { "type": "object", "properties": { "organization_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Organization ID to open." }, "mode": { "type": "string", "enum": ["org", "personal"], "description": "Initial Knowledge Dex tab to show." } } }

prompt_definitions.name: knowledge_dex_onboard
prompt_definitions.description: Guided onboarding to bootstrap your Knowledge Dex with business concepts, entities, and attributes. Interviews you about your domain, then creates entries with visual preview and approval.
